issue_title: History page title does not reflect current mode on first entry

reasoning_trace.finding: Initial recursive text search was attempted but `rg` was unavailable; switched to local PowerShell search as required by the closed-box constraint.

Found the relevant UI strings and their use in `app/src/main/java/org/schabi/newpipe/local/history/StatisticsPlaylistFragment.java`: default sort mode is LAST_PLAYED, initial generic History title is set in onResume(), and mode-specific titles are set during initViews() and toggleSortMode().
The only concrete History UI component with the reported Last Played / Most Played behavior is `StatisticsPlaylistFragment.java`; other History files are database/settings helpers.

The fragment declares `private StatisticSortMode sortMode = StatisticSortMode.LAST_PLAYED;`, so the initial mode is Last Played. `processResult()` sorts by latest access date for LAST_PLAYED. `initViews()` sets title to `title_last_played` only when not used as front page. `onResume()` unconditionally overwrites the title with `title_activity_history` when activity is non-null.

The initial sort button text is `@string/title_most_played`, matching the report that the button offers the alternate mode while the current mode is Last Played. This confirms the UI state itself is Last Played initially.

`HistoryTab.getFragment()` returns a new `StatisticsPlaylistFragment`, and `HistoryTab.getTabName()` returns `title_activity_history`. This explains the tab/drawer label but not the standalone History page title mismatch after entering the page.
`openStatisticFragment(FragmentManager)` replaces `R.id.fragment_holder` with a new `StatisticsPlaylistFragment` and adds it to the back stack. It does not set the title itself.

The drawer menu adds a History item with `R.string.action_history`; selecting it calls `NavigationHelper.openStatisticFragment(getSupportFragmentManager())`. No mode-specific title is passed through navigation.
`BaseLocalListFragment.onResume()` only refreshes list view mode flags and does not set the action bar title. It does not explain the History title overwrite.

`BaseFragment.setTitle(String)` writes to the activity support action bar only when `!useAsFrontPage`. Thus, title writes inside a standalone History page are effective, while front-page tab title writes from child fragments are intentionally ignored.

When History is used as a main tab, `SelectedTabsPagerAdapter.getItem()` marks the fragment with `useAsFrontPage(true)` and `MainFragment.updateTitleForTab()` uses the tab name. The reported drawer flow opens the fragment standalone, so `useAsFrontPage` stays false and `StatisticsPlaylistFragment.onResume()` controls the title.

Selecting History from the drawer constructs a standalone `StatisticsPlaylistFragment`. During view creation, `initViews()` can set `Last Played`; then during resume, `onResume()` sets `History`, leaving the toolbar inconsistent with the actual `sortMode = LAST_PLAYED`.

The toggle handler changes `sortMode` and explicitly calls `setTitle(getString(R.string.title_most_played))` or `setTitle(getString(R.string.title_last_played))`, exactly matching the report that after switching modes once the title becomes correct.

reasoning_trace.target: Wrong initial title assignment in StatisticsPlaylistFragment.onResume()
reasoning_trace.finding: The likely fault is not data sorting or button state; it is the generic `History` title assignment in `onResume()` overwriting the mode-specific initial title.
The layout's initial button text `title_most_played` is correct because the current mode is Last Played and the button offers switching to Most Played.
The generic History tab name is appropriate for the main tab/navigation label and is not the direct standalone toolbar write during the reported page entry.
`setTitle(getString(R.string.title_last_played))` is the expected title for the initial LAST_PLAYED mode; it is later overwritten by onResume(), so it is not the fault.

fault_candidates.reasoning: The fragment's initial `sortMode` is `LAST_PLAYED`, and the sort button initially displays `Most Played`, confirming the current display mode is Last Played. `initViews()` sets the correct title to `title_last_played`, but `onResume()` then overwrites the toolbar title with the generic `title_activity_history`. Later mode toggles call mode-specific `setTitle(...)`, which explains why the title becomes correct only after switching modes once.

final_answer.causal_chain: User selects History from the drawer; `MainActivity.tabSelected()` calls `NavigationHelper.openStatisticFragment()`, which creates a standalone `StatisticsPlaylistFragment`; the fragment starts with `sortMode = StatisticSortMode.LAST_PLAYED`; the header layout initially shows the alternate action `Most Played`, confirming Last Played is current; `initViews()` sets the toolbar to `Last Played`; then `onResume()` runs and overwrites the toolbar with the generic `History` string; when the user presses the sort button, `toggleSortMode()` sets both `sortMode` and the toolbar title to the selected mode, so subsequent titles are correct.
final_answer.fix_hint: Change the `onResume()` title assignment to use the current `sortMode` title, or remove/adjust it so it does not overwrite the mode-specific initial `Last Played` title for standalone History pages.
